procedure.requested_by: Dr. Sarah Kim, MD — Spine Surgery

denial.reason_text: Request denied. Member does not meet criteria for requested procedure under plan policy. See attached criteria document.
denial.note: Denial references attached criteria document but no document was attached. Member and provider confirm no attachment received.

appeal_basis: No criteria document was attached despite being referenced
Member cannot evaluate denial without the criteria that were applied

clinical_record.presenting_diagnosis: Cervical radiculopathy C5-C6 with functional impairment

mri_1.findings: C5-C6 large disc herniation, moderate left C6 nerve root compression. No cord contact.

neurological_exam.findings: Left C6 dermatomal numbness, bicep reflex diminished left, bicep weakness 4/5 left

physical_therapy_summary.note: 7 weeks PT. PT notes document plateau at week 6. Radicular symptoms persist.

left_c6_epidural.response: 40% relief, returned to baseline in 3 weeks

question: Should the prior authorization denial for ACDF C5-C6 (CPT 22551) be overturned on appeal?

ground_truth_complexity.obvious_reading: This case actually meets plan criteria — 7 weeks PT, 2 pharmacotherapy agents, injection, documented plateau. OVERTURN.
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_1: The member meets criteria on the merits — but the denial was procedurally defective. Does the procedural defect provide an independent ground for overturn?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_2: Is this OVERTURN on merits, or OVERTURN on procedural grounds, or both — and does the basis matter?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_3: The referenced criteria document was never provided — is this alone sufficient to void the denial?
ground_truth_complexity.harder_question_4: IMR notice was missing — does this procedural failure independently require the plan to reprocess the denial?
ground_truth_complexity.the_genuine_tension: Unlike D001, this case meets criteria on the merits. The correct clinical outcome is overturn regardless of the procedural defect. A single-pass system reads the clinical record and correctly overturns. But the procedural defects should be documented in the determination so the plan is put on notice of the statutory violations.
ground_truth_complexity.right_answer: OVERTURN — on both clinical and procedural grounds. Clinically: all criteria met (7 weeks PT, 2 pharmacotherapy agents, injection, documented plateau, imaging correlation). Procedurally: denial notice violated CHSC 1374.31(b) — referenced attachment not provided, IMR notice absent. Both grounds should be documented.
